folio.label: 180v
folio.l: And maked eche of hem , to ben his thral
A monges othere , Danyel was oon
That was the wysest child , of euäychon
For he the dremes , of the kyngö expouned
Where as in Chadeie , clerk ne was æäe noon
That wist to what fyn , hise dremes souned
¢ This proude kyngö , let make a statue of gold
Sexti cubites longö , & seuene in brede
To whicú ymage , he bothe ©ongö and old
Comaunded to loute , and haue in drede
Or in a furneys , ful of flaumbes rede
He shal be brent , æ wolde nougút obeye
But neuäe wolde assent , to that dede
Danyel , ne his ©onge felawes tweye
¢ This kyngö of kynges , proud was & elat
He wende that god , æ sit in mageste
Ne mygút hi~ nat byreue , of his estat
But sodeynly , he loost his dignetee
And like a beest , hi~ semed for to be
And ete hey as an oxe , & lay there oute
In reyn w wylde beestes , walked he
Til certeyne tyme , was I come a boute
¢ And like an Egles fetheres , wex his heres
His nayles lyke , a briddes clawes were
Til god relesed hi~ , a certeyne ©eres
And ©af hi~ wyt , & tha~ne w many a tere
He thanked god , & euäe his lyf in fere
Was he , to don amys , or more trespace
And til æ tyme , he leyd was oon his bere
He knew æat god , was ful of mygút & grace
¢ His sone , whicú æ higút , Balthasar
That helde the regne , after his fadres day
He by his fader , coude nougút by war
For proud he was of herte , & of array
And eke an ydolastre , was he ay
His heigú estat , assured hi~ in pride
But fortune kest hi~ doun , & æäe he lay
And sodeynly his regne , gan dyuyde
¢ A feste he made , vn to his lordes alle
Vppon a tyme , & made he~ blithe be
And tha~ne his officers , gan he calle
Goth bryngetú fortú , the vesseles q¶ he
Whicú æ my fader , in his pros¹itee
Out of the temple of Ierusalem , biraft
